sub_roles: Technical Instructor – Full Stack / DSA / Python
required_skills: Proficiency in one or more programming languages (Python, JavaScript, Java, C++, or C) at an instructional level
Teaching Data Structures and Algorithms (DSA) including arrays, linked lists, trees, graphs, sorting, and dynamic programming
Curriculum design and lesson planning for structured coding courses or bootcamps
Live coding demonstrations and pair programming in a classroom or virtual setting
Code review and constructive technical feedback for beginner-to-intermediate level learners
Problem-based learning and project-driven pedagogy for programming skill development
Assessment design for coding courses including assignments, coding challenges, and capstone projects
Ability to explain abstract computational concepts (recursion, time complexity, OOP) to non-technical or beginner audiences
preferred_skills: Experience teaching web development (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Node.js) or full-stack development bootcamps
Familiarity with competitive programming platforms and coaching students for coding contests (ICPC, Codeforces, LeetCode)
Knowledge of machine learning or data science fundamentals for teaching introductory AI/ML courses
Online live instruction experience using collaborative coding tools and virtual classroom platforms
Mentoring students through real-world project builds, GitHub portfolio development, and job placement readiness
Understanding of age-appropriate tools for teaching coding to K-12 students (Scratch, MIT App Inventor, block-based coding)
Experience with flipped classroom models, asynchronous coding assignments, and autograder integration
responsibilities: Design and deliver structured coding lessons covering programming fundamentals, data structures, algorithms, and software development best practices
Conduct live coding sessions with real-time demonstrations, interactive problem-solving exercises, and immediate student Q&A
Create progressive coding assignments, mini-projects, and capstone challenges that build learner competency from syntax basics to full application development
Provide detailed code reviews and individualized feedback on student submissions to reinforce clean coding practices and algorithmic thinking
Teach Data Structures and Algorithms (DSA) concepts including time and space complexity analysis, recursion, dynamic programming, and graph traversal
Mentor students through end-to-end project development, guiding architecture decisions, debugging approaches, and deployment on platforms like GitHub
Develop and maintain course materials including slides, coding templates, README guides, and autograded problem sets for self-paced and cohort learning
Track student progress using LMS dashboards and coding platform metrics, identifying learners who need additional support or accelerated challenge
Prepare students for technical interviews and coding assessments by conducting mock interviews, whiteboard sessions, and LeetCode-style problem walkthroughs
Collaborate with curriculum teams to continuously update course content to reflect current industry tools, frameworks, and hiring trends in software development
